idea: Differential Attention: split heads into excitatory/inhibitory pairs, take difference of two softmax maps to cancel attention noise
evidence: ICLR 2025 paper (arxiv 2410.05258) reports 25-30% loss improvement at same param count; modded-nanogpt community confirmed gains
notes: Requires splitting Q/K into two groups per head, computing two softmax maps, subtracting. Adds learnable lambda scalar. Should be compatible with existing QK-norm and RoPE.

idea: Multi-Latent Attention (MLA): low-rank KV compression via latent bottleneck, reduces VRAM allowing larger batch or model
evidence: DeepSeek-V2/V3 paper; 93% KV cache reduction; multiple open-source implementations available
notes: Compresses K,V through shared low-rank latent. Frees VRAM headroom. Main benefit is inference but training VRAM savings could allow batch_size=8 or deeper model.

idea: Sparse attention gate: sigmoid gate per head fed by first 12 dims of residual, modulates attention output to reduce BOS sink
evidence: modded-nanogpt PR#117 (-55 steps WR); already have ve_gate infrastructure that's similar
notes: Current ve_gate gates value embeddings. This would gate the full attention output instead. Could be combined with or replace ve_gate.

idea: NorMuon: normalize Muon update by applying norm step to attention params at stacked shape before reshape
evidence: modded-nanogpt PR#144 (-15 steps/-0.7s WR); note: "adding negligible value to gates and attention" per discussion
notes: Small change to Muon step. Low risk, low reward. Try after architecture changes.
